_meta.description: Per-umpire HR multiplier applied to the env component of scoreBatter. 1.0 = neutral. Any umpire NOT listed resolves to _default (1.0), so this is purely additive — populating it only helps.
_meta.range: 0.93-1.07. Effect is intentionally tiny: the multiplier hits only the env component (25% of the score), so the max score swing is ~+/-1.75%.
_meta.method: v1 conservative, cross-year-validated. Per-ump HR/Game (2024) -> multiplier vs the league-average ump, deviation compressed to 30% and clamped to +/-0.07. Then filtered to ONLY umpires whose 2024 HR-direction AGREES with their 2025 Runs/Game direction (a cross-season consistency guard that drops single-year noise). 22 of ~90 active umps cleared the filter; the rest stay neutral 1.0. See server/tools/compute_umpire_factors.py.
_meta.todo: Replace with our own internal join (scoredBatters x homerersByGame x homePlateUmpire) once we have ~3+ months of reconciled data, and re-derive every 30 days alongside the calibration loop. Keyed by boxscore official fullName.
sourceCandidates: Internal: scoredBatters x homerersByGame x umpire join, once we have history
